title: Intensely Crispy Portuguese Brussels Sprouts Skillet
description: A winey, crispy, and flavorful Portuguese dish featuring Brussels sprouts, perfect for a hangover cure, made in a cast-iron skillet with a robata-grill touch.
instructions: Preheat the robata-grill to medium-high heat.
In a large cast-iron skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the Brussels sprouts to the skillet, cut side down, and cook for 5 minutes, until browned.
Add the red wine, chicken broth, salt, black pepper, and paprika to the skillet, and stir to combine.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated robata-grill and cook for 10 minutes, until the Brussels sprouts are tender and caramelized.
Remove the skillet from the grill and stir in the lemon juice.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley, if desired.
Serve the Brussels sprouts hot, straight from the skillet.
